summary: Live-applied ADR 0326 on the rebased `codex/ws-0326-live-apply` worktree by making shard files under `workstreams/` the authored source of truth, regenerating the active-only `workstreams.yaml` compatibility surface, absorbing the concurrent `ws-0333-service-uptime-recovery` mainline change as an archive shard during rebase, and validating the new registry flow locally while preserving the protected `VERSION`, `changelog.md`, top-level `README.md` summary, and `versions/stack.yaml` updates for a separate exact-main integration step.

verification.observed: `uv run --with pytest --with pyyaml python -m pytest tests/test_workstream_registry.py tests/test_canonical_truth.py tests/test_workstream_surface_ownership.py tests/test_interface_contracts.py -q` returned `27 passed in 2.97s`.

`./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards workstream-surfaces data-models generated-portals` passed after the rebased branch declared the evidence paths and refreshed the generated registry, ADR index, diagrams, and platform manifest as needed, and the follow-on `./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards workstream-surfaces` pass confirmed the final receipt paths are owned correctly.

`./scripts/validate_repo.sh agent-standards workstream-surfaces data-models generated-docs generated-portals` exercised the generated-docs lane and failed only because `scripts/canonical_truth.py --check` correctly reported the protected `changelog.md` release-bookkeeping delta that this workstream branch is intentionally not allowed to write.

After the remote replay surfaced stale generated truth, `uv run --with pyyaml --with jsonschema python scripts/platform_manifest.py --write` refreshed `build/platform-manifest.json` and the follow-on `--check` passed cleanly.

`make remote-validate` hit a real build-host capacity issue because the managed workspace root was out of space, then fell back locally as designed. In that fallback, `agent-standards`, `workstream-surfaces`, `schema-validation`, `policy-validation`, `iac-policy-scan`, `alert-rule-validation`, `type-check`, and `dependency-graph` all executed, but the run still ended partial because `schema-validation` first caught the stale platform manifest and `ansible-syntax` timed out under the large concurrent validation load.

verification.check: Merge safety on latest mainline truth
verification.observed: The branch rebased onto `origin/main@d36f1b1d92180aab4ec32e911e82d574338aa7fd`, imported the newly merged `ws-0333-service-uptime-recovery` archive shard, regenerated `workstreams.yaml` from shard source instead of hand-merging the generated file, and passed `git diff --check` afterward.

notes: This is a branch-local live-apply receipt for a repo-coordination ADR; it intentionally leaves `VERSION`, `changelog.md`, the top-level `README.md` summary, and `versions/stack.yaml` untouched on the workstream branch.

Merge-to-main still needs the protected release bookkeeping on `main`, plus the final ADR metadata promotion from `Implemented on workstream branch` to `Implemented` with the first integrated repo version.

The remote builder currently cannot create a workspace in its managed workspace root because the build host is out of disk space; the remote validation proof therefore relies on the documented local fallback path for this run.

The local fallback remote replay also hit an `ansible-syntax` timeout while another workstream was exercising the same remote-validation infrastructure concurrently, so the remote result is preserved as partial rather than overstated as a clean pass.
